sanskrit: अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ता मोहजालसमावृताः। प्रसक्ताः कामभोगेषु पतन्ति नरके शुचौ॥
transliteration: aneka-citta-vibhrāntā moha-jāla-samāvṛtāḥ / prasaktāḥ kāma-bhogeṣu patanti narake śucau
literal_meaning: aneka = many; citta = mind/thoughts; vibhrāntāḥ = bewildered; moha = delusion; jāla = net/web; samāvṛtāḥ = enveloped; prasaktāḥ = attached/clinging; kāma = desire; bhogeṣu = enjoyments; patanti = fall; narake = into hell; śucau = unclean
contextual_meaning: A mind fragmented by endless cravings becomes entangled in its own projections; the more it chases pleasure to escape inner chaos, the deeper it sinks into a self-created psychological hell.
psychological_state: Compulsive over-consumption driven by unacknowledged inner void; dissociation from consequences; narcissistic defense against feelings of worthlessness.
trigger_scenario: doom-scrolling shopping apps at 2 a.m., chasing the dopamine hit of the next purchase while credit-card debt mounts and relationships fray.
anchor_text: Scattered desires weave a private hell; still the mind to walk free.
